comment_text:
Honestly, I haven’t watched it yet and it’s been a couple years since I last played a DMC game, even though I used to be a fan of the series. Played almost every game (did not play the 5 tho) and even watched the old animation when I was into anime and stuff. Ngl I even liked the 2013 game. People complained a lot of Dante’s hair, but the gameplay was tight! But I’m not really here for the cartoon or the game itself, but for Evanescence’s new song. Listen to this just makes me feel like a high schooler again. Back when I used to like edgy songs and edgy games a lot =D

Anyway, even though, for me, since I was 18, DMC is an anagram to Detroit Metal City, Devil May Cry still had a really special place in my life, back when I was into hack n slash like Ninja Gaiden, Metal Gear Rising, GoW, Dante’s Inferno[…] so I’ll probably check it later. It seems to be nice and I’m glad the series keep strong.

Plus, am I the only one who thought the guy in the beginning was going to start his monologue with “Gentlemen, it appears that Dethklok…”?? The meeting, dark room, weird people looking at a big screen, it’s just too much of a coincidence.

comment_text:
i'm at episode 7. The anime sucks for me as a long time fan of the franchise, but it also its good moments. The First 3 episodes were very good and I enjoyed them along with the antagonist, who is ruthless and cunning, but after the third episode something started to bug me out, so here's what I think they did wrong:
1. The anime isn't a sequel, isn't a prequel or anything in between. It is it's own thing, which to me is a lost opportunity to showcase some of the empty time lapses between the games, but hey, at least they stick to the source material... kinda.
2. The Lady's character is too annoying and hard to like. She's like this vulgar indestructible terminator girl boss protagonist, which makes no sense to remain unharmed in more than half of these encounters. At moments she feels more powerful than Dante himself, which is absurd, considering he's half demon with super strength agility and speed. Not to mention her complete overhaul of an origin story, which has nothing in common to the original. I hate her.
3. The anime is called Devil may cry after Dante's demon hunting business. By all means HE IS the franchise, but in this anime, he feels more like a side character, rather than the main protagonist. I bet you my life, that Lady has equal or more screen time than him.
4.(1) The politicization of the entire production is what really keeps it down in the mud for me to enjoy it like the games. The message is loud and clear. All americans are bad for wanting to protect their realm from the devil invaders. 
4.(2) * little spoilers next*  There are like... civilian demons in this anime. Like wtf... These civilian demons were struggling to survive the hells and were forced to be refugees in our world and somehow now the Americans are bad for wanting to eradicate them along with the bad demons. What a load of BS.
